title: Abstract
content: Nitrogen-doped TiO 2 nanotubes (N-TNTs) have been synthesized via a hydrothermal method using hexamethylene tetramine (HMT) and TiCl 3 as precursors.Photocatalytic activity was characterized by XRD, SEM, TEM, BET and UV-vis DRS.N was doped into the TiO 2 lattice, it extended the absorption region of TiO 2 to visible light.Post-heating TNTs at 300-500 C showed a maximum red-shifted at 500 C with a band gap energy of 3.09 eV.The visible light activity of N-TNTs was evaluated using CO 2 reduction.The total organic carbon (TOC) yield of N-TNT500 was 14530.0molg-catal.following 12 h of illumination.The yield is much higher than those of N-TiO 2 and TNTs.

title: Experimental
content: Degussa P-25 TiO 2 was commercially available, which was a mixture of 80 anatase and 20 rutile.Its BET surface area was about 50 m 2 g -1 .Titanium(III) chloride(TiCl 3 , 20 ww solution in 2 N Hydrochloric acid), hexamethylene tetramine (HMT 99) were used as starting materials without any further purification.Other chemicals were all reagent grade quality.All aqueous solutions were made up in de-ionized and doubly distilled water. (1) The N-TiO 2 powder was prepared by mixing 2 g HMT, 5 ml of 20 wt. TiCl 3 solution and 5 ml of distilled water.The mixture was placed into a Teflon autoclave of internal volume of 60 cm 3 .The autoclave was heated to start hydrothermal reaction at 230 C for 10-30 min.The powder products was separated by centrifugation, washed with distilled water and acetone three times, respectively, then vacuum dried at 60 C for 20 h.The products was marked N-TiO 2 . (2) The N-TNTs were prepared by N-TiO 2 powder.N-TiO 2 powders were ultrasonic suspended in 40 ml of 10 molL NaOH solution for 20 min and transferred to a Teflon autoclave.The autoclave was closed tightly and kepted at 155 C for 28.5 h.Then the precipitates were collected by centrifugation, washed with 0.1 molL HCl until pH reached ca 2.5, and washed with stirring for 2 h at room temperature.The precipitate was washed with distilled water until pH reached ca 7. The products were dried at 90 C, followed by the calcination at 300 C, 400 C, 500 C in air for 2 h, respectively.The samples obtained were labeled as N-TNT300, N-TNT400 and N-TNT500. (3) The TNT was prepared by Degussa P25.0.5 g P25 and 40 ml of 10 molL NaOH solution were ultrasonic mixed for 20 min, then hydrothermal treated at 155 C for 49 h.After the same dry and calcination process as above, the TNT was successfully prepared and labeled as TNT300, TNT400 and TNT500, respectively. The phase compositions of the samples were determined by X-ray diffraction analysis (XRD.Shimadzu XD-1) using graphitemonochromized CuK radiate ion (30 kV, 30 mA).The size and shape of the nanoparticles were observed by scanning electron microscope (SEM.Hitachi S-4100) and transmission electron microscope (TEM.JEOLJEM-2010).A diffusive reflective UV-vis spectrophotometer (DRS) was employed to measure the UV-vis absorption and estimate the bandgap of the catalysts.The specific surface area and pore structure of the prepared catalysts were measured by adsorption desorption of nitrogen using a Thermo Electron Corporation Sorptomatic 1990 and evaluated by the BET and BJH methods, respectively. Photocatalytic reaction was carried out in a quartz reactor.Catalyst nanotube (150 mg) was suspended in 100 ml of 0.1 N NaOH aqueous solution.Illumination was performed with a visible-light of 500 W tungsten-halogen lamp for 12 h.The reactor was tightly closed during reaction and stirred continuously by a magnetic stirring bar to prevent sedimentation of catalyst, then CO 2 was bubbled through the reactor for at least 45 min to purge air and saturate the solution.The yields for the formation of methanol, formaldehyde and formic acid were determined with UV-vis spectrophotometer or gas chromatograph equipped with FID detector and the HP5 column for methanol and ethanol analysis.Helium was used as a carrier gas. Blank reaction was conducted to ensure that hydrocarbon was the product of photo-reduction of CO 2 and eliminated surrounding interference.The following conditions were employed for three bland tests, respectively.(1) vis-illumination, no catalyst, CO 2 ; (2) dark, catalyst, CO 2 ; and (3) vis-illumination, catalyst, N 2 .No product was detected in the above three blank tests.004)and (106) (JCPDS 21-1272).Peaks at 2 of 27.4and 40.9are corresponded to rutile.In N-TiO 2 powder, rutile and anatase-type crystal grains with diameter of about 10 nm coexist.The diffraction peaks of N-TiO 2 powder and N-TNT are different, implying the preparation of nano-tube.Fig. 1(b,c,d) shows that rutile is disappeared.N-TiO 2 powder is a mixture of anatase(49) and rutile(51), as the TNTs are completely anatase.It means that it is the post-heat treatments that convert the rutile to anatase via the formation of titanate.For the titanate structures, there should be peaks around 2 of 10corresponding to the d-spacing of the layered structure 23,24.Three characteristic peaks positioned at 2 of 24, 28and 48in the diffraction patterns of nanotubes, have been assigned to the diffraction of titanates.Meanwhile, the content of nitrogen in N-TNTs was conducted by CHN analysis, and the result revealed that 0.73mass of nitrogen was contained in the sample N-TNT300.N atoms substitute lattice oxygen atoms in the TiO 2 structure, altering the band-gap of the TNT to the visible light region.N-doped atoms narrow the band-gap of TiO 2 and thus make it responsive to visible light irradiation.Fig. 1 (e,f,g) shows the XRD images of TNTs, in which the rutile is also disappeared.The transferring of particles to nanotubes decreased the rutile content sharply.While samples being post-heated showed extremely sharp diffraction peaks, indicating good crystallinity and large particle size.After hydrothermal treatment with NaOH and treatment with HCl, new peak appears at around 25 o in TNT corresponding to anatase TiO 2 peak, around 48corresponding to H 2 Ti 2 O 5 H 2 O is also observed on these samples, suggesting the transformation of titanate to titania after the treatments.As showed in Fig. 1, the intensity of peak at 48is higher as the post heating treatment temperature goes from 300 C to 500 C, demonstrating that TNT500 has better tube shape than TNT300.The diffraction intensity of TNT500 is stronger and narrower than that of TNT300, implying the enhancement of crystallization of the anatase phase and the growth of crystallites.Zhang et al. 25 had found that while NTA was disposed at high temperature, Orthorhombic NTA would transferred to anatase, at the same time it would produce mass oxygen space bounding with single electron, so the light of the catalyst absorb range was increased.

title: Results and discussion
content: The interstitial diffusion of N into the TiO 2 lattice, forming Ti-N bond, can strongly modify the nanotube lattice.As given in Table 1, the calculated lattice constants a and c deviate slightly from the bulk (a 3.781 A; c 9.498 A) value of anatase.The particle size is calculated using Scherrer's formula. As can be seen from Table 1, the S BET shifts are slightly increased as the post-heating temperature goes from 300 C to 500 C.Generally, surface area is an important factor that influences the rate of the photo-catalysis reaction.The surface area, the pore volume and the crystallinity of catalysts play important roles in determining their photocatalytic ability.Table 1 shows that the BET surface areas of TNTs are higher than that of TiO 2 particle's.The increased surface area allows more CO 2 to be absorbed onto the surface of the photocatalyst and increase the photocatalytic rate.The result is consistent with the observation by SEM images in Fig. 2. SEM results confirmed the tubular structure of N-TNT and TNT.It is believed 26,27 that during hydrothermal treatment with NaOH solution, titania nanotubes were generated after the treatment of TiO 2 crystals in acidic solution.Kasuga et al. 26 thought that TiO 2 nanoparticles from sludge undergo delaminating process due to an attack of excess sodium cations, producing two-dimensional nanosheets.These nanosheets could grow at high reaction temperature and curlup into nanotubes with narrower and uniform size distribution due to high surface energy.The post-heating temperature has a strong effect on the structure and morphological features of the resulted samples.Fig. 2 shows the SEM images of N-TNT and TNT.SEM images revealed that the structure of N-TNTs were stable after heating at 500 C disagreed with results of Nishijima et al. 28 the same consequence was verified by Okour 29. Fig. 3 shows the TEM images of N-TNT500 and TNT500.It shows that N-TNT500 and TNT500 are tubular structures.Fig. 3 indicates that portion of TNT500 exists as polycrystallites.N-TNT500 shows a clearer and more tubular morphology than TNT500.The nanotubes of N-TNT500 are two-layer tubes, length of 40-60 nm, inner diameter of 8 nm and outside diameter of 12 nm.In addition, the ends are opening, which is beneficial for increasing the surface area.The increase of surface area enabled to adsorb more CO 2 onto the surface and got more reduction products.In TNT500, Nano-particles are formed when the post-heating temperature is 500 C.The tube structure is destroyed while the short rod-like particles are formed.The sample of TNT500 consisted of a mixture of rod-like and tube powders.Compared with the XRD pattern of Fig. 1, it might be estimated that doping N rise the temperature of post-heating.Doping N can hold the tube structure of titania under higher temperature. The UV-vis diffuse reflectance spectra of the samples are shown in Fig. 4. The TiO 2 (P25) showed no absorption of visible-light, due to the large band-gap energy calculated as 3.8 eV.The optical absorption edge of the samples shows an appreciable shift to the visible light region after N doping.Kubelka-Munk function versus the band gap energy is shown in inset of Fig. 4. The extrapolation of the modified spectra to zero absorption determines the band gap energy (BGE) of the nanotubes.N-TNT500 showed a BGE of 3.09 eV.The band gaps of the samples can be calculated from the intercept of UV-vis spectra using the following equation: Eg 1240 and the results are given in Table 1.All the N-TNTs showed good visible-light absorption in the range of 400-800 nm and possessed two absorption edges around 410 and 580 nm, indicating the formation of N-Ti bonding in the N-TNTs.The first absorption edge around 410 nm is related to the original structure of titania.The second absorption edge should be related to the newly formed N 1s orbits caused by the nitrogen doping in the molecular structure 13.Specially, the N-TNT500 shows the most obviously absorption of visible-light.In addition, physical-absorbed water also helps the absorption of visible-light.According to Antonio et al. 30,31, dealing with TNT under higher post-heating temperatures, physical-absorbed water is removed and consequently the OH group density in the nanotubular structure drops.Moreover, the nanotubular titania edge transition red-shifts and an absorption band in the visible region appears.According to Yu et al. 32, the modification of TiO 2 by non-metal elements(S,N) can alter the crystal and electronic structure.More N adsorbed on TiO 2 or incorporated into the lattice (substituting oxygen atoms) of TiO 2 would induce a band-gap narrowing between the valence band and the conduction band, which make the excitation of the catalyst under visible-light irradiation possible. Seeing from the N 2 adsorption-desorption isotherms of titanate nanotubes prepared under different post-heating temperature, the samples showed the type IV with type A hysteresis loops according to the Brunauer-Deming-Deming-Teller(BDDT) classification, which is typical of cylindrical pores structure.The surface properties of TNTs were presented in Table 1.As can be seen from Table 1, the BET specific surface area increased along with post-heating temperature.The lowest BET surface area was observed for sample heated at 300 C (156.34 m 2 g), whereas the highest surface area was measured in case of 500 C (165.83m 2 g).Similarly, the total pore volume was increasing from 0.77 to 1.02 cm 3 g for TNT300 and N-TNT500, respectively.total products had increased obviously after N was added into TNT.The total organic carbon (TOC) can reach 14530.0molg-catal.The effect of irradiation time on the reduction of CO 2 was investigated with visible light of radiation for periods of 0-48 h as showing in Fig. 5.There are three products were obtained, formic acid, formaldehyde and methanol, in which formic acid was the main product.Contact between TNT and N atom generally involves a redistribution of electric charge.Doping of N into TiO 2 , N had two existence states: N atoms substitute for lattice oxygen atoms and gap N atoms.The energy state of N atoms substitute for lattice oxygen atoms is existed on edge of valence band, while the energy state of gap N is at the forbidden band.At the same time, doping of N might produce oxygen space.The N2p introduced by N doping appeared at forbidden band of TiO 2 33,34.N atoms substitute for lattice oxygen atoms in the TiO 2 structure, which alter the band-gap of the TNT to the visible light region.N-doped atoms narrow the band-gap of TiO 2 and thus make it responsive to visible light irradiation 19.While TNT was disposed at high temperature, Orthorhombic TNT would transferred to anatase, at the same time it would produce mass oxygen space bounding with single electron, so the light of the catalyst absorb range was increased.It was found that single phase anatase could be obtained in the TNTs.The heating temperature could influence the BET specific surface area.Usually, with increasing post-heating temperature, the BET specific surface area decreased.The photocatalytic activity strongly related with the specific surface area.However, in this work, the specific surface area did not decreased obviously after heating at 500 C, the photocatalytic activity did not decreased also.This might be due to the N atoms doping, they were stably combined with Ti forming bond of Ti-N, thus minimizing the combination probability of photo-induced electron-hole pairs.In addition, the crystallinity was also increased by calcination.

title: Conclusions
content: Bare and loaded TiO 2 nanotubes were prepared by hydrothermal method.N-TiO 2 powder is a mixture of anatase and rutile, while the TNTs are completely anatase, which indicates that the post-heat treatments convert the rutile to anatase via the formation of titanate.N-TNTs display new diffraction peak of Ti-N, implying broken Ti-O bond and new formed Ti-N bond.N atoms substitute lattice oxygen atoms in the TiO 2 , which alter the band-gap of the TNT to the visible light region.The structure of N-TNTs was stable after being heated at 500 C.N-TNTs markedly improved the visible light photocatalytic capabilities of CO 2 reduction, and the sample obtained by hydrothermal treatment and post-heating at 500 C displayed the highest level of photo-activity.CO 2 could be reduced to HCOOH,HCHO,CH 3 OH etc. among which the yield of formic acid was up to 13882.5 molg-catal. Inhibiting the recombination of photo-generated electro and hole, the N doping was responsible for this phenomenon.The photocatalytic activity was several times higher than that of titania powder. corresponded to anatase (101), (004)and (106) (JCPDS 21-1272).Peaks at 2 of 27.4and 40.9are corresponded to rutile.In N-TiO 2 powder, rutile and anatase-type crystal grains with diameter of about 10 nm coexist.The diffraction peaks of N-TiO 2 powder and N-TNT are different, implying the preparation of nano-tube.Fig.1(b,c,d) shows that rutile is disappeared.N-TiO 2 powder is a mixture of
